summary: The Step 3 verification bundle has been reconciled against the current workspace state and the recorded host-execution artifacts. No successful direct-backend BYOK rerun against http://127.0.0.1:8001 is traceable from the persisted evidence. The six transcript files under the Step 3 evidence root still contain only curl transport failures, the dedicated post-save /api/auth/me proof file is still absent, and the only recorded host execution in the backend Step 2 artifact stopped at a shell-glob cleanup error before the live probes were run.

consistency_pass.duplicate_path_report: A naive rg --files invocation echoed duplicate lines because both the directory and parent path were searched together. The filesystem itself currently contains six unique transcript files.
reasons: The verification bundle now points only at paths that exist or are explicitly marked missing.
No persisted transcript contains an HTTP status line proving a successful live request.
The required dedicated authenticated_get_me_after_byok.txt proof is absent.

checks.name: bundle paths point at the declared evidence root
checks.details: All expected transcript paths resolve under docs/verification/evidence/2026-04-19-byok-step3.

checks.name: persisted transcript count matches declared expectation
checks.details: The workspace contains 6 unique transcript files, while the bundle requires 7 including authenticated_get_me_after_byok.txt.

checks.name: persisted transcript contents prove live HTTP responses
checks.details: Each persisted transcript still contains curl: (7) Failed to connect to 127.0.0.1 port 8001 rather than HTTP status lines and JSON bodies.

checks.name: recorded host execution reached the live probe sequence
checks.details: tmp/verification/developer_back-backend-step2-20260419115724688.json records only the initial cleanup command, which failed with zsh glob expansion before startup or curl probes executed.

checks.name: recorded follow-up commands were persisted with raw results
checks.details: The backend Step 2 artifact includes follow-up commands, but no raw stdout/stderr/exit captures exist for startup, register, BYOK save, authenticated retrieval, or /api/auth/me.

checks.name: dedicated post-save /api/auth/me proof is present and linked
checks.details: docs/verification/evidence/2026-04-19-byok-step3/authenticated_get_me_after_byok.txt is missing, so the end-to-end BYOK normalization proof cannot be traced.

backend_step4_host_result.last_feedback: ABORT: chmod succeeded, but I do not have enough context about the original task to determine whether any follow-up shell command is needed.

final_assessment.blocking_reason: The current workspace evidence still reflects the pre-rerun failure state, and the recorded backend host-execution artifacts do not show a completed live verification sequence that produced traceable raw HTTP outputs.
final_assessment.next_required_action: Execute the recorded Step 3 live verification command set against the Rust backend on 127.0.0.1:8001, persist raw stdout/stderr/exit evidence for startup and all seven probes including authenticated_get_me_after_byok.txt, then rerun Step 4 completeness validation.
